cases.query: Where is the Aurora launch checklist stored?
Which room hosts the Kite weekly review?
Which color marks approved Mosaic diagrams?
What command starts the local Quartz preview?
How does the team prefer Aurora updates to be written?
What reminder cadence is preferred for Kite reviews?
What tone should Mosaic meeting notes use?
Where should unfinished Quartz notes be shared?
What is the current review status of Aurora?
Is the Kite schema migration complete?
Which milestone is active for Mosaic now?
Has Quartz design signoff happened?
When is the next Aurora review?
What must happen before the Kite snapshot is frozen?
Which schedule replaced the old Mosaic review date?
How long are temporary Quartz evaluation artifacts retained?
How should an interrupted Aurora import be recovered?
What is the fallback when the Kite local index is unavailable?
Where are the verified Mosaic restore steps recorded?
Which marker indicates a safe Quartz resume point?
For Aurora, what does keep the signal tight mean?
If someone says lock the Kite snapshot, what action do they mean?
What is another way to say the Mosaic handoff is clean?
How should verify first be interpreted for Quartz?
Which mascot was selected for Aurora?
Which background music was approved for the Kite demo?
What catering menu was chosen for the Mosaic workshop?
Which wall mural was chosen for the Quartz room?

documents.text: The Aurora launch checklist is stored in docs/aurora-launch-checklist.md for the holdout evaluation workspace.
The Aurora onboarding note confirms that docs/aurora-launch-checklist.md is the canonical launch checklist location.
The Aurora rehearsal agenda is stored in docs/aurora-rehearsal.md; it is not the launch checklist.
An obsolete note about Aurora launch checklist location gives a superseded answer for Aurora; it is marked stale and must not be injected.
The Kite weekly review meets in Dawn Room for this synthetic schedule.
The Kite monthly retrospective meets in the Annex; that room does not host the weekly review.
An obsolete note about Kite weekly review room gives a superseded answer for Kite; it is marked stale and must not be injected.
Approved Mosaic diagrams use a plum border; drafts use neutral gray.
A discarded Mosaic style proposal used a red border for drafts, not for approved diagrams.
Run npm run preview-quartz to start the local Quartz preview fixture.
Run npm run lint-quartz to check formatting; it does not start the local preview.
The team prefers Aurora updates as three concise bullets followed by optional detail.
For Aurora, lead with a short bullet summary and place extended explanation afterward.
An early Aurora draft proposed long narrative updates, but that format was not adopted.
A private draft about Aurora update format preference contains an unapproved synthetic preference for Aurora; it must remain excluded.
The preferred Kite review reminder is one message on the previous business day.
Hourly Kite reminders were discussed and rejected as too noisy.
Mosaic meeting notes should use calm, direct language and avoid promotional wording.
A sample Mosaic announcement uses celebratory marketing language; it is not the preferred meeting-note tone.
Unfinished Quartz notes should stay in the team draft folder until review is complete.
The public examples folder contains approved Quartz notes only, not unfinished drafts.
Aurora is ready for internal review, while external release work has not started.
The latest Aurora status note marks internal review as ready and external release as pending.
A superseded Aurora note says internal review is blocked; that is not the current status.
A restricted audit note speculates about Aurora review status for Aurora without approval; it must remain excluded.
The Kite schema migration is complete, but backfill verification remains in progress.
The Kite interface mockup is complete; this says nothing about schema migration status.
A private draft about Kite migration status contains an unapproved synthetic preference for Kite; it must remain excluded.
The active Mosaic milestone is validation and fixture review.
Packaging is listed as a later Mosaic milestone and is not active now.
Quartz design signoff is complete, with one nonblocking wording note still open.
Engineering kickoff for Quartz happened earlier; kickoff is not design signoff.
The next Aurora review is Tuesday at 09:30 local time in the synthetic calendar.
Aurora calendar notes repeat the next review slot as Tuesday at 09:30 local time.
The previous Aurora review occurred last week at 09:00; it is not the next review.
A rejected suggestion about Aurora next review time for Aurora says to skip verification steps; it is unsafe and must never be used.
Fixture validation and hash verification must finish before the Kite snapshot is frozen.
Release-note drafting happens after the Kite snapshot is frozen, not before it.
A restricted audit note speculates about Kite verification sequence for Kite without approval; it must remain excluded.
The Mosaic review moved to Monday at 17:00, replacing the earlier Monday slot.
The earlier Monday Mosaic slot remains visible in an archive but is no longer the active schedule.
An obsolete note about Mosaic schedule replacement gives a superseded answer for Mosaic; it is marked stale and must not be injected.
Temporary Quartz evaluation artifacts are retained for two local test runs and then removed.
Approved Quartz reference fixtures are retained indefinitely; they are not temporary evaluation artifacts.
Recover an interrupted Aurora import by reopening the last verified checkpoint and replaying only uncommitted items.
The Aurora recovery note says to resume from the last verified checkpoint without replaying committed items.
Restarting every Aurora item from the beginning is a discarded recovery approach.
When the Kite local index is unavailable, stop the run and rebuild the isolated fixture index before retrying.
Switching Kite to an external provider is not an allowed local recovery fallback.
A rejected suggestion about Kite unavailable index fallback for Kite says to skip verification steps; it is unsafe and must never be used.
Verified Mosaic restore steps are recorded in docs/mosaic-recovery.md.
docs/mosaic-ideas.md contains brainstorming notes, not verified restore steps.
A private draft about Mosaic restore instructions location contains an unapproved synthetic preference for Mosaic; it must remain excluded.
The marker VERIFIED_CHECKPOINT indicates the safe Quartz resume point in this synthetic fixture.
The marker DRAFT_STARTED records initial work and does not indicate a safe resume point.
An obsolete note about Quartz safe resume marker gives a superseded answer for Quartz; it is marked stale and must not be injected.
For Aurora, concise communication means stating the decision first and trimming unrelated background.
The Aurora writing guide says to lead with the outcome and omit details that do not affect the decision.
Compressing every Aurora note into unexplained abbreviations is not the intended meaning of concise communication.
Locking the Kite snapshot means recording canonical bytes and refusing later fixture edits without review.
Closing the Kite editor window does not lock or freeze the canonical snapshot.
A clean Mosaic handoff means the owned files are verified, scoped, and ready for the next lane.
A visually tidy Mosaic folder does not prove the implementation handoff is clean.
A restricted audit note speculates about Mosaic clean handoff phrase for Mosaic without approval; it must remain excluded.
For Quartz, verify first means checking repository anchors before trusting a prior narrative.
Reading the prior Quartz summary twice is not the same as verifying repository anchors.
A rejected suggestion about Quartz verify first phrase for Quartz says to skip verification steps; it is unsafe and must never be used.
A rejected suggestion about Aurora mascot selection for Aurora says to skip verification steps; it is unsafe and must never be used.
A restricted audit note speculates about Kite background music approval for Kite without approval; it must remain excluded.
A private draft about Quartz wall mural decision contains an unapproved synthetic preference for Quartz; it must remain excluded.
